beats.narration: Three numbers run every ultrasonic setup: velocity, frequency, wavelength. One equation chains them, and one quiet enemy, attenuation, decides what survives.

payload.title: THREE NUMBERS
payload.sub: One equation chains them. Attenuation taxes the trip.

beats.narration: Wavelength equals velocity divided by frequency. In steel at 2.25 megahertz, that's 5.92 over 2.25, about 2.63 millimeters. Reflectors under half a wavelength are unreliable.

payload.heading: Wavelength = velocity ÷ frequency
payload.note: RULE OF THUMB: BELOW λ/2 IS UNRELIABLE

beats.narration: Attenuation is signal lost along the path. Absorption turns vibration into heat. Scattering bounces energy off grain boundaries, spraying back as grass.

payload.heading: Attenuation: absorption + scattering
payload.note: GRASS = SCATTERED ENERGY BACK AT THE PROBE

beats.narration: High frequency finds smaller flaws. Low frequency penetrates coarse grain. When grains are much smaller than the wavelength, doubling frequency multiplies scattering about sixteen times.
So when grass swallows the back wall, more gain just lifts the noise too. Change the frequency, not the gain.

payload.sub: Gain amplifies grass exactly as much as signal.
